name: setFlagShow
description: Shows or hides the Milky Way galaxy rendering. When enabled, the band of our galaxy appears across the sky.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: `true` to show the Milky Way, `false` to hide it.

alternative: Use `core.getStelProperty('MilkyWay.flagMilkyWayDisplayed')` to read the current state.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getFlagShow
description: Returns whether the Milky Way galaxy is currently displayed.
returnDescription: `true` if the Milky Way is visible, `false` otherwise.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setIntensity
description: Sets the intensity (brightness) of the Milky Way rendering. Values above 1.0 make it appear brighter, below 1.0 dimmer.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getIntensity
description: Returns the current intensity (brightness) of the Milky Way rendering.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var intensity = MilkyWay.getIntensity();
core.debug("Milky Way intensity: " + intensity);

name: setSaturation
description: Sets the color saturation of the Milky Way rendering. `0.0` = grayscale, `1.0` = natural colors, >1.0 = more vivid colors.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getSaturation
description: Returns the color saturation of the Milky Way rendering.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var saturation = MilkyWay.getSaturation();
core.debug("Milky Way saturation: " + saturation);

name: setColor
description: Sets the color tint for the Milky Way rendering. White `(1, 1, 1)` gives natural colors; other values create color casts.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values between 0.0 and 1.0.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getColor
description: Returns the current color tint applied to the Milky Way rendering.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var color = MilkyWay.getColor();
core.debug("Milky Way color: RGB(" + color[0] + ", " + color[1] + ", " + color[2] + ")");
